title: Right at Your Door
one_star: ★ "A marriage stress-test disguised as a low-budget LA panic nightmare, and somehow the marriage loses."
five_star: ★★★★★ "Sealed my husband outside during a gas leak once. Didn't realise this movie was a how-to guide."

title: Hidden
one_star: ★ "The only way this movie should be watched is split into 10 parts with subway surfers covering half the screen."
five_star: ★★★★★ "The twist absolutely EATS!!!! i was having a fine time for most of it but when the reveal happens OOHHHHH SUDDENLY ITS ALL WORTH IT"

title: Carriers
one_star: ★ "Could have been called 'How To Be An Asshole at The End Of The World' and saved everyone 84 minutes of their lives."
five_star: ★★★★★ "I hate this fucking movie and every god damn thing about it but I've watched it four times so make of that what you will."

title: It Comes at Night
one_star: ★ "I can't tell what was worse — never finding out what comes at night, or the aspect ratio."
five_star: ★★★★★ "Anyone else losing their entire fucking mind"

title: Await Further Instructions
one_star: ★ "The writer of this film should be sent to prison. Not metaphorically. Actual prison."
five_star: ★★★★★ "A Christmas movie about a racist family being eaten by their television. I will be revisiting this every December."

title: Coherence
one_star: ★ "The Blair Witch Project for insufferable yuppies who own too many wine glasses."
five_star: ★★★★★ "If there are a million different realities, I have slept with your wife in every single one of them."

title: Pontypool
one_star: ★ "Hilarious that a film about radio DJs can't even get the vocals on the right frequency. Truly committed to the bit."
five_star: ★★★★★ "What if Night of the Living Dead was a locked-in War of the Worlds radio play starring Kawartha Lakes' Howard Stern? Perfection."

title: The Platform
one_star: ★ "I was with it and then it ended and now I'm mad. I wanted to throw up and I didn't even get an explanation. So dumb."
five_star: ★★★★★ "This is the most fucked up Mukbang I've ever seen and I genuinely cannot stop thinking about it."

title: Exam
one_star: ★ "Eight people locked in a room fail to ask a question for 90 minutes. Truly the most realistic job interview ever filmed."
five_star: ★★★★★ "SAW for people who love riddles and there's no gore at all, which means it's somehow more disturbing than SAW."

title: The Bar
one_star: ★ "Starts as a clever absurdist siege thriller and ends as a loud, boring descent into a sewer. Literally."
five_star: ★★★★★ "People trapped in a Madrid bar after a shooting. Devolved into full Lord of the Flies within 20 minutes. This is peak cinema."

title: Circle
one_star: ★ "50 people slowly eliminate each other with no explanation and I eliminated myself from the couch after 30 minutes."
five_star: ★★★★★ "What a shitty prize. You get to live on an earth populated by the orphans, pregnant women, and other assholes humanity voted to keep."

title: Would You Rather
one_star: ★ "The good thing about this film is it goes quickly. The bad thing is it exists."
five_star: ★★★★★ "My favourite part was when she forgot she had been stabbed. Method acting in reverse."

title: Fermat's Room
one_star: ★ "WHAT HAPPENS ON THE GODDAMN BOAT?! This film's worst sin is attempting intelligence and landing at kindergarten."
five_star: ★★★★★ "My guy was going to kill four people and himself over math. It's not that deep bro. And yet I respect him completely."

title: Level 16
one_star: ★ "Timely and interminable. A Handmaid's Tale riff that forgot to include anything happening."
five_star: ★★★★★ "A very clever little Black Mirror riff that made me feel genuinely unsafe in my own bedroom and I live alone."

title: The Incident
one_star: ★ "What was that incident?! The incident was this movie being made and nobody warning me."
five_star: ★★★★★ "A sci-fi horror masterclass in breaking your heart and stealing your sanity. The weight of infinity before your eyes. I am not okay."

title: Pandorum
one_star: ★ "I've never been more confused in my life and I once took an Ambien and watched Tenet."
five_star: ★★★★★ "Basically two films in one and both of them slap. Criminally underseen. Dennis Quaid deserved a sequel and a raise."

title: The Void
one_star: ★ "This warmed-over Lovecraft nonsense is exactly the dull fate this protagonist deserves, and somehow also me for watching it."
five_star: ★★★★★ "Grim, forlorn dankness, extraterrestrial gods, cultists with knives, and XTREME Carpenter worship. This is everything. I am fed."

title: Breathing Room
one_star: ★ "Found this in a 10 for $1 used DVD bin. Ten cents feels like maybe a bit much."
five_star: ★★★★★ "A not-as-good version of Exam, which is a compliment, because Exam is incredible and this one still broke my brain on a Tuesday."

title: The Hole
one_star: ★ "The DVD cover says 'super scary. brilliant.' which is obviously a lie written by someone who has never met either of those things."
five_star: ★★★★★ "Thora Birch locked her friends in an underground bunker to steal a boy and it almost worked. Respect the commitment to the bit."

title: The Bunker
one_star: ★ "The shit I will sit through for Tobin Bell and Tony Todd. And this is where that journey ends."
five_star: ★★★★★ "The whole film is one long Zoom call about a bioweapon and somehow that's exactly what I needed on a Friday night."
